timeline.label: Q1 2026: net income $274.1M (+87% YoY); diluted EPS $5.41; strong Global Housing and Connected Living growth
Issued $300M 5.55% senior notes due 2036; redeemed all $175M 6.10% senior notes due 2026 — term out debt at lower coupon
FY2025 results: net income $872.7M (+15%); Global Housing Adjusted EBITDA $858.7M (+28%); Global Lifestyle $801.3M (+4%)
Completed exit from Global Preneed segment; portfolio now two operating segments (Global Lifestyle and Global Housing)
Accelerated share repurchases; diluted share count declined from 53.2M (FY2023) to 51.1M (FY2025)
Expanded Connected Living mobile device solutions globally; mobile attach became majority of Lifestyle revenue (53% FY2025)
Rebranded as Assurant; sharpened focus on housing and lifestyle specialty insurance via B2B2C partner distribution
Spun off from Fortis; listed on NYSE as AIZ — specialty insurance and extended protection franchise

industry.structure: Specialty insurance and extended protection distributed through B2B2C partnerships with carriers, lenders, retailers, and OEMs. Assurant holds leading positions in mobile device protection (Connected Living), vehicle service contracts (Global Automotive), and lender-placed homeowners insurance. Concentrated partner relationships create scale but also concentration risk.
industry.share_shift: Assurant gaining in Connected Living via global mobile carrier programs and financial-services cross-sell; Global Housing benefiting from lender-placed policy growth and lower catastrophe years; traditional extended service contract mix declining as mobile attach rises.
industry.trend: Mobile device lifecycle services and embedded insurance growing; lender-placed housing sensitive to catastrophe seasons and mortgage volumes; investment portfolio yields support net investment income ($527M FY2025); debt refinanced at lower coupons.
